title: Calyptranthes zuzygium
taxonomy: Calyptranthes zuzygium is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It belongs to the genus Calyptranthes, which is a group of flowering plants commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The family Myrtaceae is a large and diverse group of plants that includes eucalyptus, guava, and clove. Calyptranthes zuzygium is native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific, where it grows as a shrub or small tree. The specific classification of Calyptranthes zuzygium is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Rosids, Order: Myrtales, Family: Myrtaceae, Genus: Calyptranthes, Species: C. zuzygium.

The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been utilized to treat various health conditions. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Calyptranthes zuzygium for medicinal purposes.
